records.ety: [OF. aseürer, F. assurer, LL. assecurare; L. ad + securus secure, sure, certain. See Secure, Sure, and cf. Insure.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Assured (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Assuring.]

defs: To make sure or certain; to render confident by a promise, declaration, or other evidence.
quotes: His promise that thy seed shall bruise our foe . . . Assures me that the bitterness of death Is past, and we shall live.

defs: To declare to, solemnly; to assert to (any one) with the design of inspiring belief or confidence.
quotes: I dare assure thee that no enemy Shall ever take alive the noble Brutus.

defs: To confirm; to make certain or secure.
quotes: And it shall be assured to him.
And hereby we know that we are of the truth, and shall assure our hearts before him.

defs: To insure; to covenant to indemnify for loss, or to pay a specified sum at death. See Insure.
